label: Marketo
description: Connect to Adobe Marketo Engage via a LaunchPoint Custom Service to read and manage leads, campaigns, and marketing activities.

form.title: Connect Marketo
form.subtitle: Paste your LaunchPoint Custom Service credentials to authorize Anton.

methods.label: LaunchPoint Custom Service
methods.description: Paste your Marketo REST API endpoint URL, Client ID, and Client Secret from a LaunchPoint Custom Service. The simplest and recommended path — no OAuth browser flow required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via LaunchPoint Custom Service

Marketo's REST API uses a dedicated service credential pair (Client ID + Client Secret) that you create inside Marketo's admin area. No browser OAuth flow is needed.

### 1. Create an API-only user

1. In Marketo, go to **Admin → Users & Roles → Roles**.
2. Create a role (e.g. `Anton API Role`) with the API permissions you need (at minimum: **Access API → Read-Only Lead**, **Read-Only Activity**, etc.).
3. Go to **Admin → Users & Roles → Users → Invite New User**.
4. Create a user (e.g. `anton-api@yourcompany.com`), assign the role from step 2, and tick **API Only**.

### 2. Create the LaunchPoint Custom Service

1. Go to **Admin → Integration → LaunchPoint**.
2. Click **New → New Service**.
3. Fill in a **Display Name** (e.g. `Anton`), set **Service** to **Custom**, and pick the API-only user you just created.
4. Click **Create**.
5. Back in the LaunchPoint list, click **View Details** next to your new service.
6. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** shown in the dialog.

### 3. Find your REST Endpoint URL

1. Go to **Admin → Integration → Web Services**.
2. Under **REST API**, copy the **Endpoint** URL — it looks like `https://123-ABC-456.mktorest.com/rest`.

### 4. Paste into Anton

Enter the REST Endpoint URL, Client ID, and Client Secret below, then click **Submit**.

### Notes
- Credentials are stored locally and never sent to Anton's servers.
- If you rotate the Client Secret in LaunchPoint, re-paste it here.
- Each Marketo instance has a unique subdomain in the endpoint URL — make sure you copy from the correct Marketo subscription if you have more than one.

fields.name: rest_endpoint_url
fields.label: REST Endpoint URL
fields.description: Found in Admin → Integration → Web Services → REST API → Endpoint.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: From the LaunchPoint Custom Service detail dialog (View Details).

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID; shown in the same LaunchPoint View Details dialog.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Custom Client)
methods.description: Use your own OAuth 2.0 client credentials to retrieve a bearer token via Marketo's identity endpoint. Suitable for advanced setups where you need full OAuth token lifecycle management.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 with Marketo Identity Endpoint

Marketo uses a client-credentials OAuth 2.0 flow — there is no user-facing browser consent screen. This method collects your Client ID and Client Secret and exchanges them for a bearer token via the identity endpoint.

### Steps

1. Follow steps 1–3 from the **LaunchPoint Custom Service** method to obtain your Client ID and Client Secret.
2. Find your **Munchkin Account ID** under **Admin → Account Settings → Munchkin** — it looks like `123-ABC-456`.
3. Your identity token URL will be: `https://123-ABC-456.mktorest.com/identity/oauth/token`
4. Enter your Client ID, Client Secret, and the full identity URL below.
5. Click **Submit** — Anton will exchange the credentials for an access token automatically.

### Notes
- Marketo access tokens expire after 3,600 seconds (1 hour). Anton will re-request a token automatically before expiry.
- Marketo does not use user-delegated OAuth scopes; all permissions are governed by the API-only user's assigned role.
- If the LaunchPoint Custom Service method is available, prefer it — it is simpler and equivalent.
